questions.prompt: You enabled WAF rules in Cloudflare but they don't appear to be applying to traffic. Most likely cause?

choices.text: Your DNS record is gray-cloud (DNS-only). Cloudflare features only apply when proxy mode is on (orange cloud) — gray-cloud records resolve to your origin IP and Cloudflare doesn't see the traffic. Toggle to orange cloud and rules begin firing.

questions.explanation: Proxy mode is the on-switch. Orange cloud = traffic flows through Cloudflare, all features active. Gray cloud = Cloudflare serves the DNS record only; traffic skips the edge entirely. The most common 'why doesn't this work' on Cloudflare.

questions.prompt: Behind Cloudflare's proxy, your app reads `request.remoteAddress` for rate limiting. Every request looks like it came from a Cloudflare IP. What's wrong?

choices.text: When proxied, the connection's remote IP is Cloudflare's edge — not the user. Read the real client IP from the `cf-connecting-ip` header. Common patterns (Express middleware, NGINX trust_proxy) handle this automatically once configured correctly.

questions.explanation: Cloudflare passes the real client IP in `cf-connecting-ip` (and via `X-Forwarded-For`). Your app must read these for rate limiting, logging, and analytics. Default 'remote IP' reads see Cloudflare's edge IP and apply limits app-wide — exactly wrong.
